title: trochocephalus palaestinus
intro: Trochocephalus palaestinus, a plant species found in the Middle East, is also known as desert date or tamarugillo, belonging to the Anacardiaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been found to be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The plant has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Trochocephalus palaestinus include decoctions and infusions, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to treat skin conditions.
